email.subject: Daily Journey: Walker Miles on www.skechers.com

review.raw_markdown:
---

# Skechers.com Mobile Review — Walker Miles
**Date:** March 28, 2026 | **Yesterday's Score: 3/10**

---

## 1. Executive Summary

Well, I went through the whole thing again on my phone and I gotta say — it's better in spots, but that cart page is still a disaster. The Men's navigation is actually pretty clean once you get into the menu, the Slip-ins category landed me exactly where I wanted, and the product page was easy to read. But I never got any sign I was logged in, a popup hit me right away asking for my email (I'm already a Plus member, come on), and when I tried to view my cart? Blank white screen. Nothing. Like the lights went out. Can't buy what I can't see.

---

## 2. Business Impact Score: **4/10**

**Up 1 point from yesterday.** Navigation and product browsing improved, but the cart failure is still a purchase-blocker. Any improvement in discovery is completely undone the moment a customer tries to check out.

---

## 3. What's Working

**Men's Category Navigation is Clean**
That side menu is well done. Big readable text, good spacing between items, easy to tap. And "Hands Free Slip-ins" is right there as a quick-link at the bottom — I didn't have to dig. That's exactly what I want.

**Hands Free Slip-ins Category Page Delivers**
258 results, clear heading, width filter buttons (Wide Fit, Extra Wide Fit) right up front. That's thoughtful. Guys my age appreciate that we have options. The description explains the technology. Solid.

**Product Page is Readable and Clear**
The product name is big, price is easy to find ($135, noted), and that BOGO 50% off reminder right below the price is well placed. The Slip-ins badge gives me confidence I'm in the right product family. Stars and reviews are there. No complaints here — this page does its job.

**Size Grid is Functional**
Full range from 7.0 to 14.0, reasonably-sized tap targets, clear layout. I appreciate seeing a Size Chart link. The error message when no size is selected ("Please select a size") is clear and visible.

**BOGO Deal is Consistently Visible**
That blue banner stays at the top throughout. I noticed it. It's doing its job as a persistent promotional reminder.

---

## 4. What's Weak

**Cart Page is a Blank White Screen — Critical Bug**
I'm not exaggerating. After going through the whole journey — finding the shoe, going to add it — the cart page loaded as a completely blank white page. Nothing. No items, no total, no buttons, no error message. Just white. This is the same failure as yesterday. If I was actually trying to buy something, I'd close the app and call my son to look it up on Amazon. This is a sale-killer.

**Login Showed No Personalization Whatsoever**
After the login step, the page looked identical to before I logged in. No "Welcome back, Walker." No "Hi, Skechers Plus member." No saved preferences, no tailored content for men my age. I have no idea if I'm actually logged in. The cart icon still shows 0. The experience feels exactly the same as if I walked in as a stranger.

**Popup Immediately Asks for My Email**
First thing that pops up after the homepage loads — "Unlock 20% OFF, enter your email." I'm a Skechers Plus member. I'm already supposed to be logged in. Why are you asking for my email? This feels impersonal and honestly a little insulting. It's noise that I have to dismiss before I can do anything.

**Homepage Hero Image Has Nothing to Do With Me**
The homepage shows a Spring Shopping Guide with a young family. Cute photo, but I'm a 62-year-old man shopping for comfort shoes. No men's content visible above the fold. No Slip-ins featured. Nothing that says "hey Walker, we know what you like."

**Search Defaults to Women's Results**
I searched "slip in" and got 1,017 results — good. But the first two products shown in my screenshot are both tagged "Women's." If I'm logged in as a male Skechers Plus member, why am I looking at women's shoes first? That's backwards.

---

## 5. Recommendations

1. **Fix the cart page. Now.** This is a blocker. A blank cart means zero conversions. Priority one, nothing else matters until this works.

2. **Show login state clearly.** Put "Hi Walker" or a member indicator somewhere visible after login. I shouldn't have to guess whether I'm logged in.

3. **Suppress the email popup for logged-in members.** If I'm already in the system, don't ask me for my email. Show me my Plus benefits instead.

4. **Default search to my gender.** I'm a logged-in male — "slip in" search results should prioritize men's products, not women's. Add a Men's filter pre-applied based on account profile.

5. **Feature Slip-ins and men's comfort content on the homepage.** Even one personalized banner or a "Recommended for You" row would go a long way toward making me feel like Skechers knows who I am.

---

## 6. Bottom Line

The middle of this journey is getting better — navigation is clean, the Slip-ins category is easy to find, and the product page is readable on a phone. But I arrived to a generic homepage, got hit with an email popup, couldn't tell if I was logged in, and ended up staring at a blank white cart. The bones are there. The execution around entry and exit points — homepage personalization and the cart — is still broken. Until that cart loads, nothing else really matters.

**Score: 4/10** (+1 from yesterday — navigation improvements noted, but cart failure persists)

---

## 7. Evidence

### Step 1 — Homepage
The first thing I see is a BOGO 50% OFF banner at the top, which is good. Below that, the Skechers logo and a hamburger MENU. Then a "Spring Style Guide! Shop Now" sub-banner. The hero image takes up most of the screen — a family photo with a young couple and kids, promoting a Spring Shopping Guide. Big "SHOP NOW" button. **Not targeted at me at all.** Nothing about men's shoes, nothing about comfort technology, nothing about Slip-ins. I'm already scrolling to find what I want. Phone usability is fine — text is readable, button is big enough. Just no relevance to my interests.

### Step 2 — Dismiss Popup
Before I can do anything, a popup covers the page asking me to enter my email for 20% off. There's also a banner saying "Free Shipping with Skechers Plus — Join Now for free." I'm already a Plus member. This is friction I shouldn't be dealing with. The close button (X) is in the top right — small, but findable. The email field and CONTINUE button are reasonably sized. Still, this whole step feels like I'm being treated as a stranger.

### Step 3 — Log In
After logging in, the screen looks identical to the homepage from Step 1. Same Spring Shopping Guide hero. Same generic layout. No confirmation I'm logged in, no greeting, no personalization change. I have to assume the login worked, but there's no feedback. The cart still shows 0 items. This step is a trust problem — I don't know if I'm actually authenticated.

### Step 4 — Men's Category
This is where things improve. The side navigation panel opens clean and clear. "MEN" is the header. Options are: Shoes, Shop by Activity, Comfort Technologies, Collections, Collaborations, Clothing & Accessories — all with right-arrow indicators. Then below, without an expand: "Hands Free Slip-ins," "Wide Fit," "Extra Wide Fit" as direct links. **This is excellent.** The text is a comfortable size, spacing between items is generous enough for my fingers. I can tap right to Slip-ins without hunting. This is the best screen in the journey.

### Step 5 — Men's Shoes (Hands Free Slip-ins)
Landed right in the Slip-ins section. Clear "Hands Free Slip-ins" heading in large text. The description explains the Heel Pillow technology — I like knowing the tech. Width filter buttons (Wide Fit, Extra Wide Fit, Medium) are right at the top. 258 results shown. Filter and Sort options. Product thumbnails beginning to appear at the bottom of the screen. This page does everything right. **Good experience.**

### Step 6 — View Product
The product name "Skechers Slip-ins: Max Cushioning Arch Fit 2.0 - Azusah" is big and readable. $135.00 is clearly priced. "Buy 1 Get 1 50% off! Applied at Cart" is displayed prominently — that's a meaningful deal and I'm glad it's visible. 4-star rating with 8 reviews is there. The Hands-Free Slip-ins badge confirms I'm in the right product. The shoe image is large. Navigation arrows to see more angles. **This page works well for me.** Only minor issue: 8 reviews is a thin sample — could use more social proof.

### Step 7 — Add to Cart
The size selection grid is clean and easy to read. Full range 7.0–14.0. I can see I didn't select a size — the red warning "Please select a size" is clear and prominent. QTY dropdown is there. "Ship it" and "Check in-store availability" options are readable. The big blue "ADD TO CART" button at the bottom is very obvious — no issue finding or tapping it. **This step works.** The forced size selection before adding is appropriate and the error message is clear.

### Step 8 — View Cart
**Blank. White. Screen.** That's all I saw. No header, no footer, no cart items, no "your cart is empty" message, nothing. The page simply did not load. This is the same failure as yesterday. I cannot stress enough how bad this is — a customer who got this far in the journey is ready to buy and the cart page shows them nothing. **Complete failure. Unchanged from yesterday.**

### Step 9 — Search Results ("slip in")
The search for "slip in" returned 1,017 results — solid breadth. The heading "SLIP IN" is large and clear. Filter and Sort options are accessible. But the first two products in my results are both labeled "Women's" — "Arch Fit Arcade - See Ya There" (Women's) and "Contour Foam - Cozy Fit" (Women's). I'm a logged-in male. Why is women's content leading my search results? The color swatches under each product are a nice touch and the prices are partially visible. But the gender mismatch undermines the personalization that should come with being a member. **Functional but not personalized.**

---

## Technical Audit

## Technical Summary

Site runs on **Salesforce Commerce Cloud (Demandware)**. The journey encountered **Kasada bot protection rate limiting** (429s on login and cart), a **Stylitics CORS misconfiguration** breaking a third-party widget, **32 axe accessibility violations** including 4 critical, and a severely over-loaded third-party script stack (~20+ scripts). LCP and CLS were not captured for any step, leaving core Web Vitals incomplete.

---

## Accessibility

**Critical (4 violations):**
- `aria-required-parent` — Homepage contains ARIA roles that lack required parent containers. Affects screen reader tree structure.
- `aria-required-children` / `aria-valid-attr-value` — View Product page has ARIA roles with missing required children and invalid attribute values. Likely broken carousel or custom listbox.
- `button-name` — Nameless `<button>` elements on View Product. Completely opaque to assistive tech.

**Serious (5 violations):**
- `link-name` — Anchor tags with no discernible text on Homepage (icon-only links without `aria-label`).
- `aria-dialog-name` / `aria-input-field-name` — Unnamed dialog and input on View Product (likely size selector modal).
- `link-in-text-block` — Men's Shoes page: inline links distinguished only by color, no underline or other non-color indicator.
- `color-contrast` — View Product fails WCAG 2 AA minimum contrast ratio.

**Moderate (12+ violations):**
- Duplicate `<main>` landmark on Homepage (`landmark-no-duplicate-main`). This is a structural defect, not just a warning.
- `<main>` and `<aside>`/complementary landmarks are nested inside other landmarks (`landmark-main-is-top-level`, `landmark-complementary-is-top-level`) — indicates incorrect template nesting.
- `landmark-unique` violation on Homepage and Men's Shoes: multiple same-type landmarks without distinguishing labels.
- `heading-order` skipped heading levels on Homepage.
- `region` — content outside any landmark on both Homepage and Men's Shoes.

---

## SEO

- `<html lang="en">` is set correctly across all pages.
- All pages use a consistent `g-local-en_US` class — locale/hreflang implementation present.
- **Kasada 429s on `/login/` and `/cart/`** — if Googlebot triggers bot protection, these pages will be uncrawlable. Kasada's crawler allowlist should be verified for Googlebot user agents.
- Full `<head>` truncated in samples; canonical tags, structured data (Product, BreadcrumbList), and `<meta robots>` could not be verified.
- Duplicate script tags (`tags.creativecdn.com` injected twice on Men's Category) indicate GTM rule misconfiguration — no direct SEO impact but signals tag governance issues.

---

## Performance

| Step | TTFB | LCP | CLS |
|---|---|---|---|
| Homepage | 24ms | — | — |
| Log In | 54ms | — | — |
| Men's Category | 54ms | — | — |
| Men's Shoes | 32ms | — | — |
| View Product | 85ms | — | — |
| Add to Cart | 85ms | — | — |
| View Cart | **188ms** | — | — |
| Search | 40ms | — | — |

- TTFB is acceptable across all steps. View Cart at 188ms is the outlier, consistent with cart session/auth overhead on SFCC.
- **LCP and CLS are absent for all 8 steps** — the audit is missing the two most CrUX-weighted Core Web Vitals. These need to be instrumented.
- **Third-party script overload in `<head>`:** ~20+ scripts loaded before first paint, including: TikTok (2 scripts), GTM (4 instances), Google Analytics (multiple IDs including a placeholder `G-1234567890`), Pinterest, Reddit, Bing, Yahoo (`ytc.js` injected **twice**), Snapchat, Facebook, Nextdoor, Taboola, Impact, Monetate (2 scripts), Quantum Metric, Bloomreach, Go2SDK, WebFontLoader. This directly degrades TBT and LCP.
- `G-1234567890` in GTM config is a placeholder/test GA4 ID firing in production.
- TrustArc injects **3 inline `<style>` blocks** at the top of `<head>` including a `@font-face` declaration for Source Sans Pro loaded from `consent.trustarc.com` — render-blocking.
- WebFontLoader (`webfontloader.js`) loaded async from `cdnjs.cloudflare.com` with `wf-*` classes on `<html>` — FOUT is managed but adds external CDN dependency.

---

## Mobile Optimization

- `--vh: 6.640000000000001px` is set inline on `<html>` via JavaScript — this is a workaround for the iOS Safari `100vh` viewport bug. The imprecise float value (`6.640000000000001`) suggests raw `window.innerHeight / 100` without rounding, which can cause subpixel rendering inconsistencies.
- `vice-managed-src` attribute on several `<script>` tags is a consent-management proxy pattern (likely OneTrust/TrustArc). Scripts fire only after consent — correct pattern for GDPR/CCPA compliance on mobile.

---

## Console & Network Errors

**429 — Kasada Bot Protection (5 console + 7 network):**
- `https://www.skechers.com/149e9513-01fa-4fb0-aad4-566afd725d1b/2d206a39-8ed7-437e-a3be-862e0f06eea3/fp?x-kpsdk-v=j-1.2.291` — Kasada SDK fingerprint endpoint returning 429 repeatedly. The automated browser session is being identified and throttled. `/login/` and `/cart/` returned 429, meaning authentication and checkout were blocked.
- Root cause: Kasada's `j-1.2.291` challenge SDK failed to establish a valid session token before requesting protected routes.

**CORS — Stylitics stylesheet blocked (2 console + 2 network):**
- `https://web-assets.stylitics.com/style.css?base=hotspots&version=1f1e003a2c` blocked by CORS policy: missing `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header on the Stylitics CDN response.
- A secondary XHR attempt for the same resource also fails (`ERR_FAILED`). The Stylitics "Shop the Look" / hotspots widget will render without styles or fail entirely. This is a Stylitics-side CDN misconfiguration that Skechers needs to report to them.

---

## Recommendations

1. **Kasada integration:** The SDK is failing to complete its fingerprinting handshake before navigating to `/login/` and `/cart/`. Review the Kasada integration timing — the `kpsdk` token must be established before protected page requests. Consider whether the challenge cadence is misconfigured for SPA-style navigation.

2. **Stylitics CORS:** File a support ticket with Stylitics to add `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://www.skechers.com` on `web-assets.stylitics.com`. As a short-term mitigation, proxy the stylesheet through Skechers' own CDN.

3. **LCP/CLS instrumentation:** Add `PerformanceObserver` for `largest-contentful-paint` and `layout-shift` entries, or enable real-user monitoring (Quantum Metric is already present — verify CWV collection is enabled).

4. **Remove/fix `G-1234567890`:** This placeholder GA4 ID is firing in production via GTM, generating bad data and unnecessary network requests.

5. **Deduplicate third-party scripts:** `ytc.js` (Yahoo) and `tags.creativecdn.com` are injected twice. Audit GTM container for duplicate trigger rules.

6. **Critical ARIA fixes — prioritized:**
   - Add `aria-label` to all icon-only links (Homepage).
   - Fix `aria-required-parent`/`aria-required-children` violations — likely a broken carousel or tab widget using incorrect ARIA roles.
   - Add `aria-label` to the size selector dialog and its inputs (View Product).
   - Replace nameless buttons with labeled equivalents.

7. **Fix duplicate `<main>` landmark:** Only one `<main>` element should exist per page. The SFCC template is likely including a component that wraps content in a second `<main>`.

8. **Round the `--vh` value:** Change `window.innerHeight / 100` to `Math.round(window.innerHeight / 100 * 10000) / 10000` or use `svh`/`dvh` CSS units where browser support allows.
